AI.result: Experiments show that adaptive agents outperform static models in filtering precision and user satisfaction.
AI.conclusion: Agent-based personalization effectively improves content moderation while maintaining user autonomy in online social networks.

title: SAGE: Multi-Agent Self-Evolution for LLM Reasoning
summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards improves reasoning in large language models (LLMs), but many methods still rely on large human-labeled datasets. While self-play reduces this dependency, it often lacks explicit planning and strong quality control, limiting stability in long-horizon multi-step reasoning. We present SAGE (Self-evolving Agents for Generalized reasoning Evolution), a closed-loop framework where four agents: Challenger, Planner, Solver, and Critic, co-evolve from a shared LLM backbone using only a small seed set. The Challenger continuously generates increasingly difficult tasks; the Planner converts each task into a structured multi-step plan; and the Solver follows the plan to produce an answer, whose correctness is determined by external verifiers. The Critic scores and filters both generated questions and plans to prevent curriculum drift and maintain training signal quality, enabling stable self-training. Across mathematics and code-generation benchmarks, SAGE delivers consistent gains across model scales, improving the Qwen-2.5-7B model by 8.9% on LiveCodeBench and 10.7% on OlympiadBench.

AI.tldr: SAGE is a self-evolving framework with four agents (Challenger, Planner, Solver, Critic) that improves reasoning in LLMs by generating tasks, planning, and verifying answers without large human-labeled datasets.
AI.motivation: To address the limitations of current methods, which rely on large datasets or lack planning and quality control, SAGE introduces a closed-loop system for stable, long-horizon reasoning.
AI.method: Four agents (Challenger, Planner, Solver, Critic) co-evolve from a shared LLM backbone, generating tasks, converting them into plans, solving them, and verifying their correctness.
AI.result: SAGE improves the Qwen-2.5-7B model by 8.9% on LiveCodeBench and 10.7% on OlympiadBench, demonstrating consistent gains across benchmarks.
AI.conclusion: SAGE offers a scalable and stable approach for improving reasoning in LLMs with minimal reliance on human-labeled data.

title: Knowledge Distillation for Large Language Models
comment: Code and data are available at: https://github.com/AlejandroParedesLT/knowledge_distillLLM
summary: We propose a resource-efficient framework for compressing large language models through knowledge distillation, combined with guided chain-of-thought reinforcement learning. Using Qwen 3B as the teacher and Qwen 0.5B as the student, we apply knowledge distillation across English Dolly-15k, Spanish Dolly-15k, and code BugNet and PyTorrent datasets, with hyperparameters tuned in the English setting to optimize student performance. Across tasks, the distilled student retains a substantial portion of the teacher's capability while remaining significantly smaller: 70% to 91% in English, up to 95% in Spanish, and up to 93.5% Rouge-L in code. For coding tasks, integrating chain-of-thought prompting with Group Relative Policy Optimization using CoT-annotated Codeforces data improves reasoning coherence and solution correctness compared to knowledge distillation alone. Post-training 4-bit weight quantization further reduces memory footprint and inference latency. These results show that knowledge distillation combined with chain-of-thought guided reinforcement learning can produce compact, efficient models suitable for deployment in resource-constrained settings.

AI.tldr: A resource-efficient framework compresses large language models via knowledge distillation and chain-of-thought reinforcement learning.
AI.motivation: To create smaller, efficient models suitable for resource-constrained settings without significant loss of capability.
AI.method: Knowledge distillation from Qwen 3B to Qwen 0.5B, combined with guided chain-of-thought reinforcement learning and 4-bit weight quantization.
AI.result: Distilled student retains 70-95% of teacher's capability across tasks and reduces memory footprint.
AI.conclusion: Combining knowledge distillation with chain-of-thought reinforcement learning yields compact, efficient models.

title: Int3DNet: Scene-Motion Cross Attention Network for 3D Intention Prediction in Mixed Reality
comment: Accepted as an IEEE TVCG paper at IEEE VR 2026 (journal track)
summary: We propose Int3DNet, a scene-aware network that predicts 3D intention areas directly from scene geometry and head-hand motion cues, enabling robust human intention prediction without explicit object-level perception. In Mixed Reality (MR), intention prediction is critical as it enables the system to anticipate user actions and respond proactively, reducing interaction delays and ensuring seamless user experiences. Our method employs a cross attention fusion of sparse motion cues and scene point clouds, offering a novel approach that directly interprets the user's spatial intention within the scene. We evaluated Int3DNet on MoGaze and CIRCLE datasets, which are public datasets for full-body human-scene interactions, showing consistent performance across time horizons of up to 1500 ms and outperforming the baselines, even in diverse and unseen scenes. Moreover, we demonstrate the usability of proposed method through a demonstration of efficient visual question answering (VQA) based on intention areas. Int3DNet provides reliable 3D intention areas derived from head-hand motion and scene geometry, thus enabling seamless interaction between humans and MR systems through proactive processing of intention areas.

AI.tldr: Int3DNet predicts 3D intention areas using scene geometry and head-hand motion cues for seamless Mixed Reality interactions, outperforming baselines.
AI.motivation: To enable proactive system responses in Mixed Reality by anticipating user actions, reducing delays, and enhancing user experience.
AI.method: Cross attention fusion of sparse motion cues and scene point clouds for direct spatial intention interpretation.
AI.result: Consistent performance up to 1500 ms on MoGaze and CIRCLE datasets, and successful visual question answering demonstration.
AI.conclusion: Int3DNet reliably predicts 3D intention areas, facilitating seamless human-MR interaction through proactive processing.

title: Learning Retrieval Models with Sparse Autoencoders
summary: Sparse autoencoders (SAEs) provide a powerful mechanism for decomposing the dense representations produced by Large Language Models (LLMs) into interpretable latent features. We posit that SAEs constitute a natural foundation for Learned Sparse Retrieval (LSR), whose objective is to encode queries and documents into high-dimensional sparse representations optimized for efficient retrieval. In contrast to existing LSR approaches that project input sequences into the vocabulary space, SAE-based representations offer the potential to produce more semantically structured, expressive, and language-agnostic features. Building on this insight, we introduce SPLARE, a method to train SAE-based LSR models. Our experiments, relying on recently released open-source SAEs, demonstrate that this technique consistently outperforms vocabulary-based LSR in multilingual and out-of-domain settings. SPLARE-7B, a multilingual retrieval model capable of producing generalizable sparse latent embeddings for a wide range of languages and domains, achieves top results on MMTEB's multilingual and English retrieval tasks. We also developed a 2B-parameter variant with a significantly lighter footprint.

AI.tldr: SPLARE uses sparse autoencoders (SAEs) for learned sparse retrieval (LSR), outperforming vocabulary-based LSR in multilingual and out-of-domain settings.
AI.motivation: To create more semantically structured, expressive, and language-agnostic sparse representations for efficient retrieval compared to existing LSR methods.
AI.method: Introduces SPLARE, a method to train SAE-based LSR models, leveraging open-source SAEs.
AI.result: SPLARE-7B achieves top results on MMTEB's multilingual and English retrieval tasks; a lighter 2B-parameter variant is also developed.
AI.conclusion: SAE-based LSR models like SPLARE outperform vocabulary-based approaches, offering better generalization across languages and domains.

title: Intelligent Co-Design: An Interactive LLM Framework for Interior Spatial Design via Multi-Modal Agents
comment: 25 pages, 20 figures; accepted for publication in the Proceedings of ACADIA 2025
summary: In architectural interior design, miscommunication frequently arises as clients lack design knowledge, while designers struggle to explain complex spatial relationships, leading to delayed timelines and financial losses. Recent advancements in generative layout tools narrow the gap by automating 3D visualizations. However, prevailing methodologies exhibit limitations: rule-based systems implement hard-coded spatial constraints that restrict participatory engagement, while data-driven models rely on extensive training datasets. Recent large language models (LLMs) bridge this gap by enabling intuitive reasoning about spatial relationships through natural language. This research presents an LLM-based, multimodal, multi-agent framework that dynamically converts natural language descriptions and imagery into 3D designs. Specialized agents (Reference, Spatial, Interactive, Grader), operating via prompt guidelines, collaboratively address core challenges: the agent system enables real-time user interaction for iterative spatial refinement, while Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) reduces data dependency without requiring task-specific model training. This framework accurately interprets spatial intent and generates optimized 3D indoor design, improving productivity, and encouraging nondesigner participation. Evaluations across diverse floor plans and user questionnaires demonstrate effectiveness. An independent LLM evaluator consistently rated participatory layouts higher in user intent alignment, aesthetic coherence, functionality, and circulation. Questionnaire results indicated 77% satisfaction and a clear preference over traditional design software. These findings suggest the framework enhances user-centric communication and fosters more inclusive, effective, and resilient design processes. Project page: https://rsigktyper.github.io/AICodesign/

AI.tldr: An LLM-based, multimodal, multi-agent framework dynamically converts natural language and imagery into 3D architectural designs, improving communication and productivity.
AI.motivation: Addressing miscommunication in architectural design due to clients' lack of design knowledge and designers' difficulty in explaining spatial relationships.
AI.method: Uses specialized agents (Reference, Spatial, Interactive, Grader) with prompt guidelines, RAG for reduced data dependency, and real-time user interaction.
AI.result: Generates optimized 3D designs with high user intent alignment, aesthetic coherence, and functionality. 77% user satisfaction and preference over traditional software.
AI.conclusion: The framework enhances user-centric communication, fosters inclusivity, and improves design processes.

title: LiveWeb-IE: A Benchmark For Online Web Information Extraction
comment: ICLR 2026
summary: Web information extraction (WIE) is the task of automatically extracting data from web pages, offering high utility for various applications. The evaluation of WIE systems has traditionally relied on benchmarks built from HTML snapshots captured at a single point in time. However, this offline evaluation paradigm fails to account for the temporally evolving nature of the web; consequently, performance on these static benchmarks often fails to generalize to dynamic real-world scenarios. To bridge this gap, we introduce \dataset, a new benchmark designed for evaluating WIE systems directly against live websites. Based on trusted and permission-granted websites, we curate natural language queries that require information extraction of various data categories, such as text, images, and hyperlinks. We further design these queries to represent four levels of complexity, based on the number and cardinality of attributes to be extracted, enabling a granular assessment of WIE systems. In addition, we propose Visual Grounding Scraper (VGS), a novel multi-stage agentic framework that mimics human cognitive processes by visually narrowing down web page content to extract desired information. Extensive experiments across diverse backbone models demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of VGS. We believe that this study lays the foundation for developing practical and robust WIE systems.

AI.tldr: The paper introduces a new benchmark (\dataset) for evaluating web information extraction (WIE) systems on live websites and proposes a multi-stage agentic framework (VGS) mimicking human cognition.
AI.motivation: Traditional WIE evaluations use static HTML snapshots, ignoring the dynamic nature of the web, which limits real-world performance. The paper aims to address this gap.
AI.method: The authors curate natural language queries from live websites, categorized by complexity, and introduce VGS, a multi-stage framework that visually grounds and extracts information.
AI.result: Experiments show VGS's effectiveness and robustness across diverse models.
AI.conclusion: The study advances practical and robust WIE systems by addressing dynamic web challenges.

title: Bi-CamoDiffusion: A Boundary-informed Diffusion Approach for Camouflaged Object Detection
comment: 10 pages, 8 tables, 4 figures
summary: Bi-CamoDiffusion is introduced, an evolution of the CamoDiffusion framework for camouflaged object detection. It integrates edge priors into early-stage embeddings via a parameter-free injection process, which enhances boundary sharpness and prevents structural ambiguity. This is governed by a unified optimization objective that balances spatial accuracy, structural constraints, and uncertainty supervision, allowing the model to capture of both the object's global context and its intricate boundary transitions. Evaluations across the CAMO, COD10K, and NC4K benchmarks show that Bi-CamoDiffusion surpasses the baseline, delivering sharper delineation of thin structures and protrusions while also minimizing false positives. Also, our model consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods across all evaluated metrics, including $S_m$, $F_β^{w}$, $E_m$, and $MAE$, demonstrating a more precise object-background separation and sharper boundary recovery.

AI.tldr: Bi-CamoDiffusion improves camouflaged object detection by integrating edge priors and unified optimization, outperforming benchmarks and state-of-the-art methods.
AI.motivation: To enhance camouflaged object detection by addressing boundary sharpness and structural ambiguity, leveraging edge priors.
AI.method: Integrates edge priors via parameter-free injection and uses a unified optimization objective balancing spatial accuracy, structure, and uncertainty.
AI.result: Surpasses baseline and state-of-the-art methods on CAMO, COD10K, and NC4K benchmarks with sharper boundaries and fewer false positives.
AI.conclusion: Bi-CamoDiffusion achieves superior performance in camouflaged object detection through effective edge integration and optimization.
